# Reviewing and Revising AI‑Assisted Drafts

Responsible use of generative AI requires systematic review and revision of its outputs. This chapter outlines practices for maintaining analytical rigor when AI tools are part of the writing or thinking process.

## Verifying factual claims

AI‑generated text may include inaccuracies or fabricated details. Any factual claim introduced through AI assistance should be checked against primary sources. Fluency and specificity are not indicators of reliability.

## Evaluating reasoning and structure

Beyond factual accuracy, AI outputs should be assessed for the quality of their reasoning. Researchers should examine whether conclusions follow from premises, whether alternatives are acknowledged, and whether key uncertainties are made explicit.

## Maintaining authorship and voice

AI assistance can blur the boundary between drafting and authorship if not handled carefully. Maintaining a clear analytical voice requires active revision and intentional integration of AI‑generated material.

## Transparency and documentation

In some research contexts, documenting how AI tools were used may be appropriate. Transparency supports accountability and helps others interpret the resulting work. At a minimum, researchers should be able to explain how AI assistance shaped their process.

## Iterative use as a discipline

Effective use of generative AI is iterative rather than one‑off. Cycles of prompting, reviewing, revising, and discarding outputs help ensure that AI remains an aid to reasoning rather than a source of unexamined conclusions.
